manufacturing_management.rationale:
2 cores because production planning vs quality/inventory are
distinct vendor markets. MES (manufacturing execution) and QMS
(quality management) are sold as separate products by different
vendors; bundling them in one umbrella under-cuts both.

psg-039.category: Smart Inspection and Management - Integrated and Smart Worksite Monitoring and Inspection

compliance_inspection.reason: AiSafety provides real-time insights for safety and compliance inspection through video analytics, but does not explicitly address maintenance-specific KPIs or analytics dashboards.

lifecycle_maintenance.reason: AiSafety is a video surveillance and analytics solution with no functional coverage of asset registration, lifecycle management, depreciation, preventive maintenance, work orders, maintenance requests, or spare parts inventory.

psg-039.summary_reason: Solution covers asset.compliance within compliance_inspection core through safety and inspection capabilities, but does not fully cover any core and provides no coverage of lifecycle_maintenance operations.

compliance_inspection.reason: SafetyCulture clearly covers inspection and compliance through its 'Smart Inspection and Management' category and 'inspections, reporting' tools, but analytics/KPIs are only implied through 'real-time data capture' and 'operational visibility' without explicit mention.

asset_management.rationale:
2 cores because PSG validation (100% partial_only against the
textbook EAM-vs-CMMS cut) showed vendors cluster around
inspection/compliance/mobile capabilities OR around
register/lifecycle/work-order capabilities — never both. This
revised split reflects the actual vendor packaging: inspection-
led specialists (carbon, e-PTW, BIM-attached compliance) vs
operations specialists (CMMS + EAM combined).

supply_chain_and_inventory.rationale:
2 cores because procurement and warehouse-side inventory are
distinct vendor markets: Coupa / SAP Ariba are procurement-only
suites; Manhattan / Cin7 are warehouse-only. PSG validation
(74% partial_only against the prior split) showed
analytics belongs with procurement, not warehouse — every
PSG sol that fulfilled analytics also fulfilled purchase
orders + vendor master. Revised split groups item-master +
stock-levels + reorder + barcoding as one operational
cluster; PO + vendor + analytics as the strategic cluster.

human_resources_management.rationale:
3 cores — the only module with 3 because PSG validation showed
33% of HR sols are single_core_full on payroll specifically
(Talenox, JustLogin payroll mode). Payroll specialists are a
distinct vendor market from HR-ops players (BambooHR-style)
and from talent suites (SuccessFactors-style). The prior
binary cut bundled payroll with HR ops, masking that
payroll-only is a real market. Splitting it out reflects how
SG SMEs actually buy: many start with payroll-only, then add
HR ops or talent as they grow.
